steps.title: Identify the decisive flaw in Text 2's critique
steps.content: Among the three shortcomings Text 2 identifies, the most relevant to Text 1's causal claim is the “lack of control groups.” Without a control group to compare against, there's no way to prove the training itself caused the outcome.

steps.title: Connect the flaw to the objection
steps.content:
The missing control group allows the objection that participants would have shown high likelihood of creating new businesses regardless of whether they received training—exactly what B states.

Choices A, C, and D concern unrelated issues: observer bias, sample size mechanics, and training duration.

Text 2's critique centers on methodological design, so its author would specifically want assurance that a control group was used to rule out this alternative explanation.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
